allow:
Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
SIDEBAR-03 + INTEL-EXPAND-2 + UI-DIFFERENTIATE-1: regulatory updates page, expanded intelligence sources, null guards

- Add Regulatory Updates page with scope filter tabs \(Federal/State/Local\), agency badges, government-themed accents
- Add regulatoryUpdates nav item to sidebar for owner, exec, compliance, platform_admin roles
- Route /regulatory-updates with route guard
- Expand intelligence-collect to 37 sources across food safety, fire safety, regulatory, weather, and industry categories
- Add null/undefined guards on all .map\(\) calls in IntelligenceHub \(affected_pillars, affected_counties, action_items, full_analysis, etc.\)
- Exclude regulatory_updates category from Intelligence Hub feed \(shown on dedicated page\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
INTEL-NULLGUARD-1: fix IntelligenceHub crash — null guards on .border lookups and all .map\(\) calls

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
INTEL-PIPELINE-1: wire live intelligence pipeline to Compliance Intelligence dashboard

Hook queries only published items \(respects RLS\), live mode shows empty states instead of demo fallback, pipeline stats via edge function, dynamic source/category breakdowns derived from published insights.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
ARCH-REVIEW-1: v1.0 canonical architecture review — 12 violations identified

9-step audit covering database schema, scoring paths, risk translation,
intelligence engine, correlation engine, module governance, and derived
vs canonical enforcement. Includes 7-day migration plan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
ARCH-IMPL-2: consolidated architecture implementation plan — 20 violations, 15-day phased execution

Extends ARCHITECTURE_REVIEW_v1.md with:
- 8 new violations \(V13-V20\) including dead compliance_score_snapshots
  table, 5th scoring engine, 5 intelligence stores, missing PDF export
- Gap analysis: reporting, jurisdiction SEO, enterprise rollup staleness
- Full schema changes \(7 phases of SQL\)
- 13 edge function modifications + 1 new \(correlation-engine\)
- 8 deterministic correlation rules
- Jurisdiction SEO architecture for 58 CA counties + 50-state expansion
- 34-step deployment order \(all additive until Day 14\)
- v1.0 Definition of Complete checklist \(28 items\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
ROLE-PERMS-1: granular role permissions management page

Two-layer permission system: role-level defaults + per-user exceptions.
Owner/Executive-only access at /settings/roles-permissions with 14 module
categories, protected permissions, confirmation modals, audit log, and
3-tier permission resolution \(user exception → org override → defaults\).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
CANONICAL-SCORING-P1: activate compliance_score_snapshots + model versioning

Phase 1 of canonical scoring stabilization \(V14 fix\):
- Migration: add model_version, scoring_engine, inputs_hash to snapshots table
- Migration: create score_model_versions registry with v1.0 seed
- Migration: add snapshot_id FK on score_calculations + 5 derived tables
- Edge function: STEP 7b writes canonical snapshot after audit trail
- SHA-256 inputs_hash for reproducibility, UPSERT on \(location_id, score_date\)
- Snapshot write is try/catch isolated — never breaks scoring response

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
EDGE-FN-MONITOR-1 + COMMAND-CENTER-1 + FACILITY-SAFETY-RENAME: edge function health monitor, intelligence command center, fire→facility rename

- Edge Function Health Monitor: /admin/system/edge-functions with health summary, registry table \(18 functions\), invocation timeline, error log, cron schedule, manual invoke
- Intelligence Command Center: /admin/intelligence with 5 tabs \(signals, game plans, platform updates, notifications, crawl health\)
- Shared invocation logger utility for Deno edge functions
- System sidebar section for platform_admin \(Edge Functions + Crawl Monitor\)
- Fire Safety → Facility Safety rename across codebase
- 3 new DB migrations \(command center tables, edge function monitor tables, data wipe\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
COMMAND-CENTER-2: upgrade Intelligence Command Center with enhanced 5-tab UI

Signal Queue: 5-section review drawer \(What Changed, AI Triage, Game Plan, Actions, Activity Log\), re-triage, create platform update.
Game Plans: 4-column kanban \(Pending/In Progress/Complete/Dismissed\), linked signal refs.
Platform Updates: validation checklist before apply, disabled button until validated.
Notifications: email preview, send test, hold for digest, inline edit.
Crawl Health: alert banner for 3+ failures, freshness matrix, run now, source toggle.
Infrastructure: URL tab sync \(?tab=signals\), auto-refresh \(30s/60s\), last-updated indicator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit --amend -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''

EOF
\)")

import type { JurisdictionProfile } from './types';

export const LA_COUNTY: JurisdictionProfile = {
  slug: 'la-county',
  name: 'Los Angeles County',
  type: 'county',
  region: 'southern_ca',
  population: '10,014,009',
  facilityCount: '38,000+',
  authority: 'Los Angeles County Department of Public Health',
  authorityAbbr: 'LACDPH',
  gradingMethod: 'letter_grade',
  gradingLabel: 'Letter Grade \(A/B/C\)',
  transparency: 'HIGH',
  cities: ['Los Angeles', 'Long Beach', 'Glendale', 'Santa Clarita', 'Pomona', 'Torrance', 'Pasadena', 'El Monte', 'Downey', 'Inglewood', 'West Covina', 'Burbank', 'Compton', 'Carson', 'Whittier'],
  heroSubtitle: 'The nation\\'s largest local health department, overseeing 38,000+ food facilities across 88 cities and unincorporated areas.',
  hook: 'LA County pioneered the restaurant letter grade system in 1998 — a model now replicated across the country. Every facility gets scored on a 100-point deductive scale, and that letter grade must be posted in the front window. It\\'s one of the most transparent food safety systems in the nation, and operators need to understand exactly how it works.',

  scoring: {
    method: 'letter_grade',
    description: 'Inspectors start at 100 points and deduct for each violation found. The final score maps to a letter grade that must be publicly displayed.',
    gradeCards: [
      { grade: 'A', range: '90-100', color: 'emerald', description: 'Facility is in substantial compliance. Minor violations only.' },
      { grade: 'B', range: '80-89', color: 'amber', description: 'Moderate violations found. Improvement needed.' },
      { grade: 'C', range: '70-79', color: 'red', description: 'Major violations present. Significant risk.' },
    ],
    pointTiers: [
      { category: 'Critical Violations', points: '4 pts each', description: 'Practices that directly cause foodborne illness \(temperature abuse, cross-contamination, improper handwashing\).', color: 'red' },
      { category: 'Major Violations', points: '2 pts each', description: 'Conditions that could contribute to food contamination \(improper storage, equipment maintenance, pest activity\).', color: 'amber' },
      { category: 'Minor Violations', points: '1 pt each', description: 'General sanitation and facility maintenance issues \(signage, floor condition, lighting\).', color: 'blue' },
    ],
  },

  violations: [
    { rank: 1, name: 'Improper Hot/Cold Holding Temperatures', points: '4 pts', color: 'red', context: 'LA\\'s warm climate makes cold-holding failures especially common. Walk-in coolers and prep line units must be verified multiple times daily.' },
    { rank: 2, name: 'Inadequate Handwashing Facilities/Practices', points: '4 pts', color: 'red', context: 'Inspectors verify soap, paper towels, warm water, and proper signage at every handwash station. Staff must be observed washing hands correctly.' },
    { rank: 3, name: 'Improper Food Storage & Labeling', points: '2 pts', color: 'amber', context: 'Date marking, FIFO rotation, and proper raw/cooked separation are checked at every inspection. Walk-in organization matters.' },
    { rank: 4, name: 'Pest Activity or Evidence', points: '2 pts', color: 'amber', context: 'Roach droppings, rodent evidence, or live pests can trigger immediate closure. LA County\\'s urban density makes pest management critical.' },
    { rank: 5, name: 'Unclean Food Contact Surfaces', points: '2 pts', color: 'amber', context: 'Cutting boards, prep tables, utensils, and equipment must be cleaned and sanitized at proper intervals with correct sanitizer concentration.' },
  ],

  notes: [
    'Scores below 70 result in a numerical "score card" instead of a letter grade. Two scores below 70 within 12 months can trigger permit suspension or revocation.',
    'Long Beach, Pasadena, and Vernon operate their own independent health departments and are NOT covered by LACDPH. Those cities have separate inspection programs.',
    'LA County uses the FOIR \(Food Official Inspection Report\) format — the same standardized form used by the California Retail Food Code \(CalCode\) framework.',
  ],

  enforcement: [
    { step: 1, title: 'Routine Inspection', description: 'Unannounced inspections 1-3 times per year based on risk category. Higher-risk facilities are inspected more frequently.', icon: 'clipboard' },
    { step: 2, title: 'Re-inspection', description: 'If significant violations are found, a follow-up inspection is scheduled within 30 days to verify corrections.', icon: 'refresh' },
    { step: 3, title: 'Administrative Hearing', description: 'Persistent non-compliance triggers a formal hearing where the operator must show cause why the permit should not be suspended.', icon: 'gavel' },
    { step: 4, title: 'Permit Suspension/Closure', description: 'Imminent health hazards \(vermin infestation, sewage backup, no hot water\) result in immediate closure until hazards are abated.', icon: 'ban' },
  ],

  publicData: [
    { name: 'Public Health Restaurant Grades', url: 'http://publichealth.lacounty.gov/eh/DSE/RetailFoodInsection/RestaurantGrades.htm', description: 'Official lookup tool for restaurant inspection grades across LA County.' },
    { name: 'LACDPH Environmental Health', url: 'http://publichealth.lacounty.gov/eh/', description: 'Main portal for environmental health services including food safety.' },
    { name: 'LA County Open Data', url: 'https://data.lacounty.gov/', description: 'Open data portal with downloadable inspection datasets.' },
  ],

  fireSafety: {
    ahj: 'LACoFD / LAFD',
    ahjDetails: 'Los Angeles County Fire Department \(LACoFD\) covers unincorporated areas and 60+ contract cities. The City of LA Fire Department \(LAFD\) covers the City of Los Angeles.',
    hoodCleaning: 'Commercial kitchen exhaust hoods must be cleaned on a schedule based on cooking volume: monthly for high-volume \(24hr/solid fuel\), quarterly for moderate, semi-annually for low-volume. Per NFPA 96 Table 12.4.',
    suppression: 'UL-300 wet chemical suppression systems required. Semi-annual inspection and certification per NFPA 96. Systems must be serviced by a licensed C-16 contractor.',
    additionalNotes: 'LACoFD issues separate fire prevention permits. Kitchen suppression system certification must be current before health department will issue food facility permit.',
  },

  insights: [
    { type: 'danger', title: 'Below-70 Trap', description: 'Two scores below 70 within 12 months can trigger permit revocation. If you\\'re trending down, invest in corrective training before the next inspection.' },
    { type: 'warning', title: 'Grade Card Visibility', description: 'Your letter grade must be displayed in the front window, visible from outside. Failure to display is itself a violation and draws inspector attention.' },
    { type: 'info', title: 'Inspection Frequency', description: 'Higher-risk facilities are inspected 2-3x per year. If your facility serves vulnerable populations or uses complex cooking methods, expect more frequent visits.' },
    { type: 'success', title: 'Transparency Advantage', description: 'LA County\\'s open data portal means consumers can look up your score online. A consistent A grade is a genuine competitive advantage in this market.' },
  ],

  dataSourceAuthority: 'Los Angeles County Department of Public Health \(LACDPH\)',
};
ENDOFFILE)

import type { JurisdictionProfile } from './types';

export const SAN_DIEGO: JurisdictionProfile = {
  slug: 'san-diego',
  name: 'San Diego County',
  type: 'county',
  region: 'southern_ca',
  population: '3,298,634',
  facilityCount: '14,000+',
  authority: 'San Diego County Department of Environmental Health',
  authorityAbbr: 'SD DEH',
  gradingMethod: 'pass_fail',
  gradingLabel: 'Pass / Fail \(No Letter Grade\)',
  transparency: 'MEDIUM',
  cities: ['San Diego', 'Chula Vista', 'Oceanside', 'Escondido', 'Carlsbad', 'El Cajon', 'Vista', 'San Marcos', 'Encinitas', 'National City'],
  heroSubtitle: "California's second-largest county uses a pass/fail system — no letter grades, no public scores.",
  hook: "Unlike LA County, San Diego does not post letter grades. Facilities either pass or fail their inspection, and detailed reports are available through the county's online portal. This means consumers rely more heavily on the binary result, and operators don't get the granular feedback that a point-based system provides. Understanding what triggers a failure is critical.",

  scoring: {
    method: 'pass_fail',
    description: 'San Diego uses a pass/fail system based on the California Retail Food Code. There are no numeric scores or letter grades. Inspectors document violations by risk category.',
    gradeCards: [
      { grade: 'Pass', range: 'No critical violations unresolved', color: 'emerald', description: 'Facility meets minimum standards. Minor violations may be noted for correction.' },
      { grade: 'Fail', range: 'Critical violations present', color: 'red', description: 'Critical violations require immediate correction. Re-inspection scheduled.' },
    ],
    pointTiers: [
      { category: 'Critical \(Red\)', points: 'Fail trigger', description: 'Imminent health hazards: improper temperatures, contamination, sewage issues, vermin.', color: 'red' },
      { category: 'Major \(Orange\)', points: 'Noted', description: 'Conditions that could lead to contamination if not corrected: storage issues, equipment problems.', color: 'amber' },
      { category: 'Minor \(Yellow\)', points: 'Noted', description: 'General maintenance and sanitation: floor condition, lighting, signage.', color: 'blue' },
    ],
  },

  violations: [
    { rank: 1, name: 'Improper Temperature Control', points: 'Critical', color: 'red', context: "San Diego's coastal-to-desert climate creates temperature challenges. Equipment failures during Santa Ana wind events are especially common." },
    { rank: 2, name: 'Cross-Contamination Risk', points: 'Critical', color: 'red', context: 'Improper separation of raw and ready-to-eat foods. Inspectors check storage order, cutting board usage, and prep workflow.' },
    { rank: 3, name: 'Handwashing Compliance', points: 'Critical', color: 'red', context: 'Complete handwash setup required: hot water, soap, paper towels, and signage. Missing any component is a critical violation.' },
    { rank: 4, name: 'Pest Evidence', points: 'Major', color: 'amber', context: "Rodent and cockroach activity triggers immediate corrective action. San Diego's proximity to the border means additional pest pressure." },
    { rank: 5, name: 'Food Contact Surface Sanitation', points: 'Major', color: 'amber', context: 'Proper sanitizer concentration and cleaning frequency must be demonstrated. Test strips must be available on-site.' },
  ],

  notes: [
    'San Diego County does not issue letter grades. The absence of a public grading system means operators must be proactive about maintaining compliance.',
    'The county publishes inspection reports online through their Food Facility Inspection portal. Savvy consumers do check.',
  ],

  enforcement: [
    { step: 1, title: 'Routine Inspection', description: 'Unannounced inspections based on facility risk category. Standard food facilities inspected 1-2 times annually.', icon: 'clipboard' },
    { step: 2, title: 'Re-inspection', description: 'Critical violations require a follow-up visit, typically within 10-30 days depending on severity.', icon: 'refresh' },
    { step: 3, title: 'Compliance Conference', description: 'Repeated failures result in a mandatory meeting with DEH management to develop a corrective action plan.', icon: 'gavel' },
    { step: 4, title: 'Permit Action', description: 'Continued non-compliance or imminent health hazards result in permit suspension and facility closure.', icon: 'ban' },
  ],

  publicData: [
    { name: 'SD County Food Facility Inspections', url: 'https://www.sandiegocounty.gov/deh/fhd/ffis/intro.html', description: 'Look up inspection results for any food facility in San Diego County.' },
    { name: 'SD DEH Food & Housing Division', url: 'https://www.sandiegocounty.gov/deh/fhd/', description: 'Main portal for food safety regulation and permitting.' },
  ],

  fireSafety: {
    ahj: 'Local Fire Authorities',
    ahjDetails: 'Fire safety is handled by individual city fire departments and the San Diego County Fire Authority for unincorporated areas. The City of San Diego Fire-Rescue Department covers the largest jurisdiction.',
    hoodCleaning: 'Commercial hoods cleaned per NFPA 96 Table 12.4 schedule: monthly for high-volume/solid fuel, quarterly for moderate, semi-annually for low-volume operations.',
    suppression: 'UL-300 wet chemical systems required. Semi-annual inspection by licensed contractor. San Diego FD requires current suppression certification for fire clearance.',
  },

  insights: [
    { type: 'warning', title: 'No Grade Safety Net', description: "Without letter grades, a single failed inspection shows as \\"Fail\\" online with no context. There's no B grade buffer — it's pass or fail." },
    { type: 'info', title: 'Online Reports Searchable', description: 'Even without grades, your inspection history is publicly searchable. Food bloggers and local media regularly pull these records.' },
    { type: 'danger', title: 'Critical = Immediate Action', description: 'Any critical violation can trigger an immediate re-inspection. Multiple critical violations may result in same-day closure.' },
    { type: 'success', title: 'Clean Record Matters', description: 'A consistent pass history with minimal violations builds trust with the DEH and may reduce inspection frequency over time.' },
  ],

  dataSourceAuthority: 'San Diego County Department of Environmental Health \(DEH\)',
};
EOF)

import type { JurisdictionProfile } from './types';

export const ORANGE_COUNTY: JurisdictionProfile = {
  slug: 'orange-county',
  name: 'Orange County',
  type: 'county',
  region: 'southern_ca',
  population: '3,186,989',
  facilityCount: '12,000+',
  authority: 'Orange County Health Care Agency',
  authorityAbbr: 'OCHCA',
  gradingMethod: 'numeric_score',
  gradingLabel: 'Numeric Score \(0-100\)',
  transparency: 'HIGH',
  cities: ['Anaheim', 'Santa Ana', 'Irvine', 'Huntington Beach', 'Garden Grove', 'Orange', 'Fullerton', 'Costa Mesa', 'Mission Viejo', 'Newport Beach'],
  heroSubtitle: 'Numeric scoring with full public transparency — every inspection score is published online.',
  hook: "Orange County uses a 100-point numeric scoring system similar to LA County, but without the letter grade requirement. Scores are publicly available online, giving OC a unique position: quantitative transparency without the grade-card mandate. Operators get detailed point breakdowns, and consumers can look up any facility's history.",

  scoring: {
    method: 'numeric_score',
    description: 'Inspectors use a 100-point deductive system. Points are deducted for each violation based on severity. Final scores are published but no letter grade posting is required.',
    gradeCards: [
      { grade: '90-100', range: 'Excellent', color: 'emerald', description: 'Facility demonstrates strong compliance. Minor issues only.' },
      { grade: '80-89', range: 'Good', color: 'blue', description: 'Some violations noted but facility is generally compliant.' },
      { grade: '70-79', range: 'Needs Improvement', color: 'amber', description: 'Multiple violations requiring correction. Increased scrutiny.' },
      { grade: 'Below 70', range: 'Unsatisfactory', color: 'red', description: 'Significant violations present. Re-inspection required.' },
    ],
    pointTiers: [

#!/bin/bash
cd "C:\\Users\\newpa\\Downloads\\evidly-demo-final\\evidly-app-main"

echo "=== 1. IMPORTS THAT SHOULD USE @shared/ ALIAS ==="
echo "\(These exist in root src/, not in vendor\)"
echo ""

SHARED_IMPORTS=\(
"hooks/api/useAvailability"
"hooks/api/useBonuses"
"hooks/api/useEquipment"
"hooks/api/useIncidents"
"hooks/api/useInsurance"
"hooks/api/useInventory"
"hooks/api/useReports"
"hooks/api/useSchedule"
"hooks/api/useSettings"
"hooks/api/useVehicles"
"hooks/useDemoGuard"
"contexts/RoleContext"
"data/deficienciesDemoData"
"data/employeesDemoData"
"data/timecardsDemoData"
"constants/serviceTypes"
"constants/reportDefinitions"
"components/shared/PageStates"
"components/dashboard/shared/constants"
\)

for imp in "${SHARED_IMPORTS[@]}"; do
  if [ -f "src/${imp}.ts" ] || [ -f "src/${imp}.tsx" ]; then
    # Count how many files import this
    count=$\(grep -r "from ['\\"].*${imp}" apps/web-vendor/src --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" | wc -l\)
    echo "@shared/${imp} — $count imports"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "=== 2. VENDOR-SPECIFIC IMPORTS \(component cross-references within vendor\) ==="
echo "\(These are copied to vendor and reference each other\)"
echo ""

# Check if these exist in vendor
VENDOR_COMPONENTS=\(
"components/equipment/BulkQRPrintModal"
"components/equipment/EquipmentDeficiencies"
"components/deficiencies/AcknowledgeModal"
"components/deficiencies/AddDeficiencyModal"
"components/schedule/DayView"
"components/employees/EmployeesList"
"components/timecards/TeamTimecards"
\)

for comp in "${VENDOR_COMPONENTS[@]}"; do
  if [ -f "apps/web-vendor/src/${comp}.tsx" ]; then
    echo "EXISTS IN VENDOR: ${comp}"
    count=$\(grep -r "from ['\\"].*${comp}" apps/web-vendor/src --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" | wc -l\)
    echo "  → Referenced $count times"
  else
    echo "NOT IN VENDOR: ${comp}"
  fi
done
EOF)
